So let's get specific here. I'll tell you exactly some of the do's and don'ts of body language and really how it works and how it impacts the person you're trying to influence. Now again, I've said this about three or four times already, I'll say it again, face to face you have only one twenty-fourth of a second before you've been judged by the way you look and the way you carry yourself. In that time you've been dissected, you've been assessed, you've been reassessed, you've been packaged, compartmentalized in this spot of either someone that they want to do business with or someone they simply don't want to talk to at all. And from that point forward, either you're reaping the rewards of having a positive image in the person's mind or you're playing catch up on trying to fight your way out of this negative situation. Now let me talk about myself for a second. For goss of whatever context I'm in, whether it's a selling situation, a training situation, speaking on stage as a keynote speech, I always make sure that I present a professional, smart, clean appearance. Well dressed, well groomed, presentable, my hair's got to be trimmed, I'm not going to have a beard and a facial hair, it simply would not be congruent with the message that I'm trying to put out there, which is a corporate message. Even if I make jokes and I'm funny, I'm always aware that that first impression of me has got to be one that they expect, which would be sort of like a Wall Street guy, a guy who is dressed as a banker. It doesn't matter whether all day long I'm not acting as a banker anymore, I have to have that first impression, that impact that causes someone's brain to blink up, everything makes sense. This guy is as I expected him to be. And seriously, many salespeople completely overlook the importance of body language as a skill to sell, as a skill to get into rapport, and they simply find themselves playing catch-up ball in a sale. They'll make a negative first impression with body language, then they can't figure out why the person is turned off, and then they're talking a mile a minute and trying every trick in the book so to speak to get themselves back into rapport. Now one thing I will tell you is that even when you're on the phone and they can't see you, body language is still important because it comes through in the tone of your voice. I spoke about this on part one of this module, how the gestures that we make affect the tonality that comes out of our mouths. So again, you want to make sure that you're moving your body and holding your body in a way that's going to be creating the right mental image in the person's mind.
